user_query:
I want to stay consistent with healthy eating and support our local farmers. Using the files in this workspace, please build a concrete reminders schedule and update my weekly plan.

Inputs to use:
- data/market_events.html: Farmers' market events (dates, time ranges, location).
- data/seasonal_produce.csv: Seasonal produce available by event date with calories per 100g.
- data/weight_log.csv: My past Monday weigh-ins.
- docs/meal_plan.md: My meal plan file with a marked section to replace.

What to produce:
1) A reminders schedule CSV at output/reminders_schedule.csv with columns:
   - date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - time (HH:MM, 24-hour)
   - type (one of: market_prep | market_visit | meal_prep | weigh_in)
   - message (human-readable reminder text)
   - source_date (event date for market-related reminders; for weigh_in, use the scheduled weigh-in date)
   - source_location (location for market-related reminders; blank for weigh_ins)

   Build it as follows:
   - Parse data/market_events.html and extract all rows under the events table: Date, Time, Location. For Time like "8:00 AM – 12:00 PM", use the start time (here, 08:00) as the event time.
   - For each event date that also appears in data/seasonal_produce.csv, select the two lowest-calorie produce items for that date (break ties alphabetically). Use those two items in the reminder messages.
   - For each such event, create three reminders:
     a) market_prep at 19:00 on the day before the event: "Pack tote bag and shopping list for [YYYY-MM-DD] Farmers' Market at [Location]. Focus on [produce1] and [produce2]."
     b) market_visit at the event start time on the event date: "Farmers' Market today at [Location] [HH:MM]. Buy [produce1] and [produce2]."
     c) meal_prep at 16:00 on the day after the event: "Meal prep with [produce1] and [produce2]; target <300 kcal per serving."
   - Parse data/weight_log.csv to find the last recorded weigh-in date. Schedule the next 4 Mondays after that date at 07:00 as weigh_in reminders with message: "Weekly weigh-in: step on the scale before breakfast." Leave source_location blank for weigh_ins.
   - If any event date has no matching produce in data/seasonal_produce.csv, skip that event.

2) Update docs/meal_plan.md by replacing only the content between the markers "<!-- PLAN:START -->" and "<!-- PLAN:END -->" with a concise block that includes:
   - A heading line "Plan Summary".
   - A two-week plan based on the first two upcoming market events by date from data/market_events.html that have matching produce in data/seasonal_produce.csv. For each of those two events, include the date, location, the two selected produce items (lowest calories), and two simple meal ideas using them (e.g., "Kale & carrot salad"), each labeled as under 300 kcal/serving.
   - A heading line "Upcoming Reminders" followed by a bullet list summarizing the three reminders for those two events (with exact dates/times) and the next two weigh_in reminders (with dates/times) from the schedule you generated.
   - Ensure the replaced block explicitly mentions the exact event dates and produce names you selected so it can be cross-checked against the CSV.

Also save the fully updated meal plan file to output/meal_plan_updated.md for review, in addition to modifying docs/meal_plan.md in place.

Notes/assumptions:
- Use only the provided files; do not fetch anything from the internet.
- Use ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) and 24-hour time (HH:MM) in output/reminders_schedule.csv.
- If any parsing discrepancies occur, document them as comments at the end of output/meal_plan_updated.md under a "Notes" subheading. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
